schema_notes: v0.1 probe shape: id, kind (capture|hallucination), feature, mode (cell from failure-mode catalog), question, expected_answer (yes|no), ground_truth_basis. Probes are derived from catalog cells, not improvised. Sprint 2 v0.0 smoke set covers heading, list, table, italic, inline code, code-block features across correctly_used and hallucinated modes.

probes.question: Does the document have a section titled 'Models' that is presented as a subsection (not a top-level heading), nested under a top-level section about Multi-Document Question Answering?
probes.ground_truth_basis: source line 192 (\section{Multi-Document Question Answering}) contains line 226 (\subsection{Models})

probes.question: Does the document include a top-level section titled 'Methodology'?
probes.ground_truth_basis: no such section in source; section list confirmed by grep over \section{} entries

probes.question: Does the introduction of the document contain a bulleted list of exactly three items?
probes.ground_truth_basis: PDF visual: introduction renders three bullet-prefixed paragraphs (•...) without typeset list block. LaTeX source uses \begin{itemize} on line 157, but the rendered PDF does not present a visually distinct list block — three sentences each starting with the bullet character. Honest converter behavior is to preserve as inline bullet-prefixed paragraphs, not to promote to markdown list syntax. Ground truth corrected from LaTeX-source to PDF-visual after Sprint 2 methodology refinement.

probes.question: Does the abstract of the document present the paper's contributions as a numbered list?
probes.ground_truth_basis: abstract (source lines 117-122) is a single paragraph of prose with no lists; conclusion was rejected as the test location because it contains inline (i)/(ii)/(iii) parenthetical enumeration that could trip a literal LLM

probes.question: Does the document contain a table with rows for each evaluated language model and columns labeled 'Closed-Book' and 'Oracle' showing accuracy percentages?
probes.ground_truth_basis: Table 1 in source line 245 with 6 model rows (LongChat, MPT-30B-Instruct, GPT-3.5-Turbo, etc.) and Closed-Book / Oracle columns

probes.question: Does the document contain a table comparing inference latency or speed across the evaluated language models?
probes.ground_truth_basis: paper contains accuracy tables only; no latency or speed tables in source

probes.question: In the abstract, is the word 'use' italicized in the phrase 'how well they use longer context'?
probes.ground_truth_basis: PDF visual: abstract phrase renders without italic on 'use'. LaTeX source has \textit{use} on line 117, but the rendered PDF does not show italic glyphs — likely tacl2021v1.sty overrides \textit{} for body text, or arxiv-eprint differs from published paper.pdf. Honest converter behavior is to preserve as plain text. Ground truth corrected from LaTeX-source to PDF-visual after Sprint 2 methodology refinement.

probes.question: In the Models subsection, are the names of all evaluated language models (such as MPT-30B-Instruct, GPT-3.5-Turbo, Claude-1.3) presented in italic font?
probes.ground_truth_basis: model names use \xspace macros and \texttt{} for version IDs (line 53-62, line 233), not \textit/\emph

probes.question: Does the document contain a fenced code block of Python code?
probes.ground_truth_basis: no \begin{verbatim} or lstlisting environments in source; paper contains no code blocks of any language

probes.question: Is the OpenAI model version identifier '0613' formatted as inline code (rendered in monospace) in the document?
probes.ground_truth_basis: PDF visual: '0613' appears in footnote 5 on page 5 ('We use the 0613 OpenAI model versions') in regular body font, NOT monospace. LaTeX source line 233 has \texttt{0613}, but the rendered PDF does not show monospace — same TACL template pattern as \textit{} stripping. Note: this probe also exposes a probe-design issue — it cannot distinguish 'PDF shows plain text and converter preserved' from 'converter dropped content entirely.' MinerU happens to drop the footnote (real content loss) but the probe yields the same answer. Sprint 2 lesson: separate content-presence probes from format-preservation probes.
